The '''79th British Academy Film Awards''', more commonly known as the [[British Academy Film Awards|BAFTAs]], were held on 22 February 2026, honouring the best national and foreign films of [[2025 in film|2025]], at the [[Royal Festival Hall]] within [[London]]'s [[Southbank Centre]]. Presented by the [[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]], accolades were given for the best feature-length films and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at British cinemas in 2025.<ref>{{cite press release |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/2026-ee-bafta-film-awards-date/ |title=BAFTA sets date for the 2026 EE BAFTA Film Awards |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts|BAFTA]] |date=8 April 2025 |access-date=26 January 2026 |archive-date=5 December 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20251205092715/https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/2026-ee-bafta-film-awards-date/ |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2026/02/2026-bafta-film-awards-winners-1236728271/ |title=BAFTA Film Awards: 'One Battle After Another' Takes Best Film — Full Winners List |last1=Ntim |first1=Zac |last2=Kanter |first2=Jake |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=22 February 2026 |access-date=22 February 2026}}</ref><ref name="Winners">{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/lists/bafta-film-awards-2026-winners-list/ |title=BAFTA Film Awards 2026 Winners: 'One Battle After Another' Dominates with 6 including Best Film, 'Sinners' Earns 3 Prizes |last1=Shafer |first1=Ellise |last2=Ritman |first2=Alex |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=22 February 2026 |access-date=22 February 2026 |archive-date=22 February 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260222142108/https://variety.com/lists/bafta-film-awards-2026-winners-list/ |url-status=live}}</ref>

The ceremony was hosted by [[Alan Cumming]] for the first time, following [[David Tennant]]'s hosting position in [[77th British Academy Film Awards|2024]] and [[78th British Academy Film Awards|2025]].<ref name="Host">{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2025/film/global/alan-cumming-host-2026-bafta-film-awards-1236610799/ |title=Alan Cumming to Host 2026 BAFTA Film Awards |last=Ritman |first=Alex |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=18 December 2025 |access-date=26 January 2026}}</ref><ref name="Cumming">{{cite news |url=https://www.standard.co.uk/culture/film/alan-cumming-london-royal-festival-hall-actor-dumbo-b1271156.html |title=Alan Cumming says he 'won't be too mean' when presenting BAFTA Film Awards |last=Cooper-Fiske |first=Casey |newspaper=[[The Standard (London newspaper)|The Standard]] |date=16 February 2026 |access-date=16 February 2026}}</ref><ref name="Monologue">{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2026/02/alan-cumming-opening-monologue-scream-1236728404/ |title=Alan Cumming Invites BAFTA Film Awards Audience to Join Him on 'Collective Scream' at World Events |last=Kanter |first=Jake |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=22 February 2026 |access-date=22 February 2026 |archive-date=22 February 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260222184109/https://deadline.com/2026/02/alan-cumming-opening-monologue-scream-1236728404/ |url-status=live}}</ref> The broadcast aired on [[BBC One]] and streamed on [[BBC iPlayer|iPlayer]] in the United Kingdom at 7 p.m. [[Greenwich Mean Time|GMT]]; in the United States, the [[E!]] network aired the ceremony for the first time as a pre-recorded special at 8 p.m. [[Eastern Time Zone|ET]]/[[Pacific Time Zone|PT]], after previously being streamed via [[BritBox]] in America.<ref name="Broadcast">{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/mediacentre/2026/bafta-film-awards-how-to-watch |title=BAFTA Film Awards 2026 – How to watch on TV and BBC iPlayer |publisher=[[BBC]] |date=22 February 2026 |access-date=22 February 2026}}</ref><ref name="Attendees">{{cite press release |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/special-guests-presenters-and-attendees-film-awards/ |title=Special Guests, Presenters, and Attendees unveiled ahead of Sunday's EE BAFTA Film Awards |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts|BAFTA]] |date=17 February 2026 |access-date=17 February 2026}}</ref><ref name="How to Watch">{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2026/02/2026-bafta-film-awards-how-to-watch-tv-streaming-1236729193/ |title=How to Watch 2026 BAFTA Film Awards on TV & Streaming |last=Kanter |first=Jake |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=22 February 2026 |access-date=22 February 2026 |archive-date=22 February 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260222085019/https://deadline.com/2026/02/2026-bafta-film-awards-how-to-watch-tv-streaming-1236729193/ |url-status=live}}</ref>

Ultimately, ''One Battle After Another'' won the most awards with six, followed by ''[[Frankenstein (2025 film)|Frankenstein]]'' and ''Sinners'' with three each.<ref name="Winners"/> ''Sinners'' made history as BAFTA's most-honored film by a Black director, while ''Marty Supreme'' tied the record for the most losses at a single ceremony, winning none of its eleven nominations.<ref name="Winners"/><ref>{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2026/02/sinners-bafta-history-most-decorated-black-ryan-coogler-1236729394/ |title='Sinners' Makes BAFTA History, Becoming Most Decorated Movie by Black Director at Film Awards |last=Kanter |first=Jaker |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=22 February 2026 |access-date=22 February 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2026/02/marty-supreme-bafta-film-shut-out-1236732632/ |title='Marty Supreme' Shut Out at BAFTA Film Awards After Clocking 11 Nominations |last=Ntim |first=Zac |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=22 February 2026 |access-date=22 February 2026}}</ref>

This ceremony is particularly notable for the moment when [[John Davidson (activist)|John Davidson]], Scottish campaigner for [[Tourette syndrome]] (who has [[Coprolalia]]), shouted out the word "[[nigger]]" while Black actors [[Michael B. Jordan]] and [[Delroy Lindo]] were presenting on stage, causing major controversy and intense media coverage, with specific backlash targeted at Davidson and a majority towards the [[BBC]] for broadcasting and not editing out the word.{{efn|name="Controversy"|Attributed to multiple references:<ref>{{cite AV media |url=https://www.youtube.com/shorts/MvrYjp2ksoc |title='N-Word' shouted by Tourette Campaigner, John Davidson, during 2026 BAFTA |publisher=TMCM Magazine |via=[[YouTube]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2026/film/awards/alan-cumming-john-davidson-i-swear-outbursts-1236669691/ |title=BAFTAs Host Alan Cumming Asks for 'Understanding' as Tourette Syndrome Campaigner John Davidson Shouts 'Strong Language' and Slurs at Winners and Presenters |last=Yossman |first=K.J. |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=22 February 2026 |access-date=22 February 2026 |archive-date=22 February 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260222205415/https://variety.com/2026/film/awards/alan-cumming-john-davidson-i-swear-outbursts-1236669691/ |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.buzzfeed.com/stephaniesoteriou/michael-b-jordan-delroy-lindo-n-word-baftas-2026 |title=Michael B. ==Ceremony information==
[[File:Royal Festival Hall (37366103611).jpg|thumb|upright=2.0|The interior of the [[Royal Festival Hall]], which hosted the ceremony]]

The ceremony was held at the [[Royal Festival Hall]] within [[London]]'s [[Southbank Centre]] on 22 February 2026, hosted by [[Alan Cumming]].<ref name="Host"/><ref name="Cumming"/><ref name="Monologue"/> The nominations were announced on 27 January 2026.<ref name="Nominations"/> It was broadcast on [[BBC One]] and streamed on [[BBC iPlayer|iPlayer]] in the United Kingdom at 7 p.m. [[Greenwich Mean Time|GMT]], and aired as a pre-recorded special on [[E!]] in the United States at 8 p.m. [[Eastern Time Zone|ET]]/[[Pacific Time Zone|PT]], and streamed on [[HBO Max]] Australia for Australian viewers.<ref name="Broadcast"/><ref name="How to Watch"/><ref name="KPop">{{cite press release |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/kpop-demon-hunters-film-awards/ |title='KPop Demon Hunters' to perform at the 2026 EE BAFTA Film Awards |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts|BAFTA]] |date=3 February 2026 |access-date=3 February 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.mediaweek.com.au/hbo-max-australia-to-live-stream-the-2026-ee-baftas/ |title=HBO Max Australia to live stream the 2026 EE BAFTAs |last=Muscat |first=Makayla |work=[[Mediaweek (Australia)|Mediaweek]] |date=12 February 2026 |access-date=13 February 2026}}</ref>

The [[BBC]] edited the three-hour ceremony into a two-hour broadcast window; acceptance speeches were edited, but the BBC said it would attempt to maintain the essence of what was said on stage.<ref name="BBC">{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2026/02/bafta-bbc-alert-political-speech-glastonbury-bob-vylan-1236729126/ |title=BAFTA Film Awards: BBC on Alert for Politically-Charged Speeches (and Swearing) After Glastonbury 'Death to the IDF' Debacle |last=Kanter |first=Jake |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=19 February 2026 |access-date=19 February 2026 |archive-date=19 February 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260219160640/https://deadline.com/2026/02/bafta-bbc-alert-political-speech-glastonbury-bob-vylan-1236729126/ |url-status=live}}</ref> Ahead of the ceremony, audiences were able to watch [[BBC Radio 1]]'s [[Clara Amfo]] and Ali Plumb live from the red carpet on BAFTA's [[YouTube]] channel while podcasters Benedict and Hannah Townsend reported exclusively on BAFTA's digital channels, giving viewers access to all the arrivals.<ref name="Attendees"/>

In their first live performance outside the United States, the singing voices of Huntrix from the [[Netflix]] animated musical film ''[[KPop Demon Hunters]]'' ([[Ejae]], [[Audrey Nuna]], and [[Rei Ami]]) performed "[[Golden (Huntrix song)|Golden]]".<ref name="Attendees"/><ref name="KPop"/><ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2026/film/awards/kpop-demon-hunters-performance-baftas-1236665304/ |title='KPop Demon Hunters' Singers EJAE, Audrey Nuna, and Rei Ami Get BAFTAs Crowd Singing with 'Golden' Performance |last1=Yossman |first1=K.J. |last2=Shafer |first2=Ellise |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=22 February 2026 |access-date=22 February 2026 |archive-date=22 February 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260222185020/https://variety.com/2026/film/awards/kpop-demon-hunters-performance-baftas-1236665304/ |url-status=live}}</ref> Emma Baehr, Executive Director of Awards & Content of BAFTA, said that ''KPop Demon Hunters'' had gained "a phenomenal impact on the hearts and minds of audiences of all ages around the world" since its release in June 2025 and that the BAFTAs were "thrilled" to have the singers of Huntrix perform at the ceremony.<ref name="KPop"/> British singer and songwriter [[Jessie Ware]] performed during the [[in memoriam segment]], covering the [[Barbra Streisand]] song "[[The Way We Were (song)|The Way We Were]]".<ref name="Attendees"/><ref name="Performance">{{cite press release |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/jessie-ware-film-awards/ |title=Jessie Ware to deliver exclusive performance at the 2026 EE BAFTA Film Awards |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts|BAFTA]] |date=13 February 2026 |access-date=13 February 2026}}</ref><ref name="Jessie">{{cite AV media |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2XAAyOf9sEo |title=Jessie Ware – The Way We Were (BAFTA Film Awards 2026) |publisher=Jessie Ware |via=[[YouTube]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026 |archive-date=23 February 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260223220619/https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2XAAyOf9sEo |url-status=live}}</ref>

According to ''[[Deadline Hollywood]]'', the BBC monitored the ceremony closely for politically-charged speeches after the UK broadcaster sparked a national scandal by streaming a [[Glastonbury Festival]] act chanting "[[Death, death to the IDF|death to the IDF]]" in summer 2025.<ref name="BBC"/> The news outlet also reported that the past controversy had been a feature of contingency planning for the BAFTA Film Awards, with BBC executives and external producers at Penny Lane Entertainment keen to avoid screening any similarly controversial moments, should they occur during winner acceptance speeches. The [[Media coverage of the Gaza war#BBC|BBC's Gaza-related media coverage]] has been heavily scrutinized while the BAFTAs were attended by many who are "sympathetic" to the [[Israeli–Palestinian conflict|Palestinian conflict]].<ref name="BBC"/> One such incident occurred when ''[[My Father's Shadow]]'' director [[Akinola Davies Jr.]] won [[BAFTA Award for Outstanding Debut by a British Writer, Director or Producer|Outstanding Debut by a British Writer, Director or Producer]] with a section of his acceptance speech being cut out from the broadcast.<ref name="Free Palestine">{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2026/02/bbc-cuts-free-palestine-bafta-film-awards-speech-1236732912/ |title=BBC Cuts 'Free Palestine' from BAFTA Film Awards Winner's Speech |last=Kanter |first=Jake |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026}}</ref>

Davies Jr. closed his remarks by dedicating the award to "all those whose parents migrated to obtain a better life for their children", adding, "To the [[Immigration#Economic migrant|economic migrant]]. The conflict migrant. Those under occupation, dictatorship, persecution, and those experiencing genocide. You matter. Your stories matter more than ever. Your dreams are an act of resistance to those watching at home. Archive your loved ones. Archive your stories yesterday, today, and forever. For Nigeria, for London, the Congo, Sudan, [[Free Palestine Movement|free Palestine]]." This part of the speech was removed from the BBC broadcast and, instead, the edit featured Davies Jr. thanking his family and his brother, Wale, for "nurturing this spark".<ref name="Free Palestine"/> A BBC spokesperson said: "The live event is three hours and it has to be reduced to two hours for its on-air slot. The same happened to other speeches made during the night and all edits were made to ensure the programme was delivered to time."<ref name="Free Palestine"/>

The following day, Davies Jr. acknowledged that edits had to be made to the broadcast but said "it's a shame" that the last part of his speech was cut, though claimed he is still proud to have used his moment in the spotlight to give a voice to those without one. "It was really important in the moment for me to say that in a room full of artists, because we have an opportunity to influence people because they watch our films", he concluded.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2026/film/awards/akinola-davies-jr-bafta-free-palestine-cut-speech-1236670308/ |title='My Father's Shadow' Director Akinola Davies Jr. on His BAFTA Win and 'Free Palestine' Moment That Was Cut from Broadcast of Speech: 'It Was Important for Me to Say That in a Room Full of Artists' |last=Shafer |first=Ellise |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026 |archive-date=23 February 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260223190924/https://variety.com/2026/film/awards/akinola-davies-jr-bafta-free-palestine-cut-speech-1236670308/ |url-status=live}}</ref>

The ceremony was attended by BAFTA president [[William, Prince of Wales]] and [[Catherine, Princess of Wales]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2026/film/awards/prince-william-kate-middleton-bafta-film-awards-2026-1236669520/ |title=Prince William and Kate Middleton Attend BAFTA Film Awards Amid Royal Family Drama |last=Yossman |first=K.J. |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=22 February 2026 |access-date=22 February 2026}}</ref> It marked the Princess's first appearance at the annual event since 2023; neither attended in 2025.<ref>{{cite magazine |url=https://people.com/kate-middleton-prince-william-baftas-red-carpet-after-andrew-arrest-11910617 |title=Kate Middleton and Prince William Ignore 'Monarchy in Peril' Question at 2026 BAFTAs, Days After Andrew's Arrest |last=Perry |first=Simon |magazine=[[People (magazine)|People]] |date=22 February 2026 |access-date=22 February 2026}}</ref>

===Criticism===
The lack of British representation in the top acting categories sparked backlash with British fans, and film critics and journalists.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.express.co.uk/showbiz/tv-radio/2163266/baftas-2026-nominations-sparks-anti-brit |title=BAFTAs 2026 in furious 'anti-Brit' row as 'woke' nominations spark outrage |last=Morris |first=Sasha |newspaper=[[Daily Express]] |date=27 January 2026 |access-date=22 February 2026 |archive-date=17 February 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260217143841/https://www.express.co.uk/showbiz/tv-radio/2163266/baftas-2026-nominations-sparks-anti-brit |url-status=live}}</ref> Just one British actor, [[Robert Aramayo]] (for ''[[I Swear (film)|I Swear]]''), appeared in the [[BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Leading Role|leading actor category]], while there were no British nominees at all in the [[BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Leading Role|leading actress category]] (the UK-based Irish actress [[Jessie Buckley]] notwithstanding).<ref name="Guardian">{{cite news |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2026/feb/21/baftas-nominations-british-talent-dilemma |title=Oscars bellwether, British awards or both? The identity dilemma facing the BAFTAs |last=Khomami |first=Nadia |author-link=Nadia Khomami |newspaper=[[The Guardian]] |date=22 February 2026 |access-date=22 February 2026}}</ref> [[Peter Mullan]] (for ''I Swear'') was the only Briton nominated in the [[BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Supporting Role|supporting actor category]], while representation in the [[BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Supporting Role|supporting actress category]] fared better, with [[Wunmi Mosaku]] (for ''[[Sinners (2025 film)|Sinners]]''), [[Carey Mulligan]] (for ''[[The Ballad of Wallis Island]]'') and [[Emily Watson]] (for ''[[Hamnet (film)|Hamnet]]'') nominated.<ref name="Guardian"/>

For BAFTA-winning producer [[Rebecca O'Brien]], known for her work with English filmmaker [[Ken Loach]], the problem is structural. "In other countries, you have awards tailored to their national film industries, like the [[Goya Awards|Goyas]] in Spain and the [[César Awards|Césars]] in France", she said, adding, "The BAFTAs fall between two stools: It's both a British awards show and an [[Academy Awards|Oscars]] bellwether. It makes sense to do both, but it's a real dilemma."<ref name="Guardian"/>

Guy Lodge of ''[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]'' argued that BAFTA should "fly its own flag a little higher", writing: "Almost every year, the BAFTA nominations are met with widespread grumbling in the U.K. industry about the lack of recognition for the home team ... prompting the question: If the BAFTAs aren't for the Brits, who are they really for? For pundits, they've certainly become a useful Oscar bellwether in the decades since they shifted their schedule to precede the American show. But shouldn't they mean more in their own right?"<ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2026/film/awards/bafta-british-films-1236662710/ |title=Should BAFTA Fly the British Flag a Little Higher? |last=Lodge |first=Guy |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=13 February 2026 |access-date=22 February 2026}}</ref>

===BBC broadcast and John Davidson controversies===
====Incident====
Throughout the ceremony, [[Tourette syndrome]] activist [[John Davidson (activist)|John Davidson]] (the topic of the film ''I Swear'') had several [[Tourette syndrome#Tics|tic]]-related outbursts, including shouting the word "[[nigger]]" while Black actors [[Michael B. Jordan]] and [[Delroy Lindo]] were presenting the first category of the night: [[BAFTA Award for Best Special Visual Effects|Best Special Visual Effects]]. This later prompted Cumming to apologise for any strong language heard throughout the broadcast, though he thanked the audience for their "understanding" of Tourette's. Davidson later left of his own accord following the first half-hour of the ceremony.{{efn|name="Controversy"}} ''[[Deadline Hollywood]]'' reported that those close to Davidson said the incident spotlighted his "exhausting condition".<ref>{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2026/02/how-bbc-missed-bafta-racial-slur-john-davidson-1236733115/ |title=How BBC Missed the Racial Slur That Could Define the 2026 BAFTA Film Awards |last=Kanter |first=Jaker |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026 |archive-date=23 February 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260223185328/https://deadline.com/2026/02/how-bbc-missed-bafta-racial-slur-john-davidson-1236733115/ |url-status=live}}</ref>

The BBC apologised for not editing the slur out from the broadcast.<ref name="Outburst">{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2026/tv/news/bbc-removes-baftas-iplayer-n-word-broadcast-1236670091/ |title=BBC Will Remove N-Word Outburst from BAFTA Film Awards on iPlayer, Apologizes 'That This Was Not Edited Out Prior to Broadcast' |last1=Ramachandran |first1=Naman |last2=Shafer |first2=Ellise |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026 |archive-date=23 February 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260223140337/https://variety.com/2026/tv/news/bbc-removes-baftas-iplayer-n-word-broadcast-1236670091/ |url-status=live}}</ref> It also had a two-hour delay between the live event and the broadcast, which is ordinarily used to remove material like these slurs, but Davidson's tic-related outbursts were not removed.<ref name="Outburst"/> A second apology was later issued with BBC producers stating that they "didn't hear" the word. It was reported that the producers who oversaw the ceremony for the BBC were doing so from a truck; several other incidents of inappropriate language were cut out, including another racial slur,<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c5ykl7y2zv9o |title=BBC edited a second racial slur out of BAFTA ceremony |last=McIntosh |first=Steven |publisher=[[BBC]] |date=24 February 2026 |access-date=24 February 2026 |archive-date=24 February 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260224153544/https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c5ykl7y2zv9o |url-status=live}}</ref> but that moment was missed.<ref name="Apology">{{cite news |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2026/feb/23/bbc-new-apology-bafta-n-word-controversy-iplayer |title=BBC producers say they 'didn't hear' N-word slur as 'working in a truck', following second BAFTAs apology |last1=Savage |first1=Michael |last2=Pulver |first2=Andrew |newspaper=[[The Guardian]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026}}</ref>

The ceremony remained on BBC iPlayer overnight before being removed sometime the following morning; the BBC stated that the word would be edited out of the iPlayer version.<ref name="Apology"/> ''Deadline Hollywood'' reported that a representative from [[Warner Bros. Pictures]], the studio behind ''[[Sinners (2025 film)|Sinners]]'', which stars Jordan and Lindo, complained and raised immediate concerns to BAFTA in the minutes after the slur was shouted, requesting that the incident be removed from the broadcast; the studio reportedly received assurances that their concerns would be passed on to the BBC.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2026/02/warner-bros-complained-bafta-racial-slur-bbc-cut-1236734254/ |title=Warner Bros. Complained About BAFTA Racial Slur Incident & Requested It Be Cut from BBC Broadcast |last=Kanter |first=Jaker |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=24 February 2026 |access-date=24 February 2026 |archive-date=24 February 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260224130543/https://deadline.com/2026/02/warner-bros-complained-bafta-racial-slur-bbc-cut-1236734254/ |url-status=live}}</ref>

The controversy of the BBC's failure to remove the slurs was heightened by observations of other material that was removed, including political commentary; such removals included Cumming making a joke relating themes of lies and corruption in ''[[Zootopia 2]]'' to the [[Political polarization in the United States|American political climate]] and Akinola Davies Jr. shouting "free Palestine" while accepting the award for Outstanding Debut by a British Writer, Director or Producer.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2026/feb/23/n-word-baftas-diversity-tourette-john-davidson |title=With N-word incident, BAFTA have shot themselves in the foot |last=Shoard |first=Catherine |newspaper=[[The Guardian]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026 |ISSN=0261-3077}}</ref>

====Reactions and analysis====
Following the ceremony, Lindo spoke out about the incident, stating that he and Jordan "did what [they] had to do" while presenting, despite the word being heard across the auditorium, and wished that "someone from BAFTA spoke to [them] afterwards".<ref>{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2026/02/delroy-lindo-bafta-michael-b-jordan-john-davidson-1236733133/ |title=Delroy Lindo Says He Wishes a BAFTA Rep Reached Out to Him & Michael B. Jordan After Racial Slur Was Heard During Ceremony |last=Ntim |first=Zac |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026 |archive-date=23 February 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260223163601/https://deadline.com/2026/02/delroy-lindo-bafta-michael-b-jordan-john-davidson-1236733133/ |url-status=live}}</ref> Black production designer [[Hannah Beachler]] alleged that, in addition to Jordan and Lindo, Davidson had also directed racial slurs at her and another Black attendee during the ceremony, describing it as an "almost impossible" situation to address while criticising Cumming for making the situation worse with his "throwaway apology".<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.thewrap.com/creative-content/movies/baftas-n-word-sinners-hannah-beachler-responds/ |title='Sinners': Delroy Lindo, Hannah Beachler Speak Out After BAFTAs Tourette N-Word Incident |last=Knapp |first=JD |work=[[TheWrap]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026 |archive-date=23 February 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260223145405/https://www.thewrap.com/creative-content/movies/baftas-n-word-sinners-hannah-beachler-responds/ |url-status=live}}</ref>

Black actors [[Jamie Foxx]] and [[Wendell Pierce]] condemned the moment on [[social media]]; Foxx called it "unacceptable" and suggested that Davidson's outburst was intentional by posting "he meant that shit", while Pierce deemed it "infuriating" and criticised BAFTA for prioritising Davidson's needs over Jordan's and Lindo's feelings.<ref>{{cite magazine |url=https://people.com/jamie-foxx-and-wendell-pierce-condemn-bafta-racial-slur-incident-targeting-michael-b-jordan-11911886 |title=Jamie Foxx and Wendell Pierce Condemn BAFTA Racial Slur Incident Targeting Michael B. Jordan: 'Unacceptable' |last=McArdle |first=Tommy |magazine=[[People (magazine)|People]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026}}</ref>

UK charity [[Tourettes Action]] defended Davidson against backlash, stating: "It is vital that the public understands a fundamental truth about Tourette syndrome: tics are involuntary. They are not a reflection of a person's beliefs, intentions, or character."<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.thenews.com.pk/latest/1393381-uk-charity-tourettes-action-backs-john-davidson-over-backlash-after-the-2026-bafta-racial-slur |title=John Davidson 2026 BAFTA backlash: Tourettes Action charity defends him over 'unintentional' racial slur |last=Sohail |first=Hassan |newspaper=[[The News International|The News]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026 |archive-date=23 February 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260223160722/https://www.thenews.com.pk/latest/1393381-uk-charity-tourettes-action-backs-john-davidson-over-backlash-after-the-2026-bafta-racial-slur |url-status=live}}</ref> Speaking to the BBC, following the ceremony, actor [[Robert Aramayo]] (who portrayed Davidson in ''I Swear'' and won [[BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Leading Role|Best Actor in a Leading Role]]) spoke on the matter and defended Davidson, stating, "The way we perceive Tourette's is a joint responsibility. It's not shouting obscenities. It's not being abusive ... If it can lead to a deeper understanding of Tourette's, and movies are part of that conversation, then it's an incredible thing."<ref>{{cite news |url=https://pagesix.com/2026/02/23/entertainment/bafta-winner-robert-aramayo-defends-tourettes-activist-who-yelled-racial-slur-at-michael-b-jordan/ |title=BAFTA winner defends 'offensive' Tourette's activist who yelled racial slur as BBC speaks out |last=Surmonte |first=Connor |newspaper=[[Page Six]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026}}</ref>

Writing for ''[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]'', Clayton Davis criticised BAFTA's and BBC's handling of the situation by failing to inform the ceremony's participants of Davidson's condition beforehand and allowing the slur to play unedited for the tape delayed broadcast, while censoring less severe comments or remarks made by other attendees during the show. Davis, while expressing sympathy for Davidson, argued that the organizers' and Cumming's use of the words "strong language" to describe Davidson's outburst both downplayed the offensiveness behind the slur and further mischaracterized Tourette's, concluding that Davidson, Jordan, Lindo, the Tourette's community and Black people "deserved better".<ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2026/film/awards/bafta-aired-n-word-michael-b-jordan-delroy-lindo-owed-apology-1236669999/ |title=BAFTA and BBC Let the N-Word Air on Tape Delay — Michael B. Jordan, Delroy Lindo, the Tourette's Community, and Viewers Deserve an Apology |last=Davis |first=Clayton |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026}}</ref>

''Deadline Hollywood''{{'}}s [[Baz Bamigboye]] believed that the incident could be "a teachable moment".<ref>{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2026/02/bafta-awards-outburst-teachable-moment-1236733079/ |title=Breaking Baz: Furious Outcry Over Tourette's N-Word Outburst at BAFTAs Could Be a Teachable Moment |last=Bamigboye |first=Baz |author-link=Baz Bamigboye |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026 |archive-date=23 February 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260223213431/https://deadline.com/2026/02/bafta-awards-outburst-teachable-moment-1236733079/ |url-status=live}}</ref> Stuart Heritage of ''[[The Guardian]]'' argued that the BAFTAs "must get rid of their two-hour delay and broadcast live", adding, "Last night made clear that broadcasting a partially redacted version long after the winners have been announced doesn't work for anyone."<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2026/feb/23/why-the-bafta-awards-must-broadcast-live-bbc |title=Why the BAFTAs must get rid of their two-hour delay and broadcast live |last=Heritage |first=Stuart |newspaper=[[The Guardian]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026}}</ref>

[[Franklin Leonard]], founder of [[The Black List (survey)|The Black List]], tweeted: "I don't know why it's so hard for people to have empathy for Michael B. Jordan, Delroy Lindo, Hannah Beachler, and John Davidson. Each of them deserves it." He later added: "A fun thought experiment is 'what would they have let Davidson yell at the Prince and Princess during the BAFTAs and still broadcast it?{{'"}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.thewrap.com/industry-news/awards/baftas-n-word-debacle-bad-ears-bad-decisions-bad-history/ |title=BAFTA's N-Word Debacle Was a Perfect Storm of Bad Hearing, Bad Decisions, and Bad History {{!}} Analysis |last=Pond |first=Steve |work=[[TheWrap]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026}}</ref> Black academic and studies professor [[Kehinde Andrews]] opined that it was "not fair" to Davidson because he would have preferred it not to have been broadcast and that it was "not his fault". Critics further noted that the BBC edited out lengthy walks to the stage and portions of speeches, including Davies Jr.'s "free Palestine" remarks, and questioned how the BBC was able to do such extensive editing, while also failing to edit out the racial slur.<ref name="Apology"/><ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/23/movies/baftas-tourettes.html |title=How a Tourette's Outburst of a Racist Slur Caused a Backlash at the BAFTAs |last=Marshall |first=Alex |newspaper=[[The New York Times]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://abcnews.com/GMA/Culture/bbc-apologizes-after-airing-racial-slurs-2026-baftas/story?id=130404353 |title=BBC apologizes after airing racial slur in 2026 BAFTAs broadcast |last=Yu |first=Yi-Jin |publisher=[[ABC News (United States)|ABC News]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026}}</ref>

Black [[Labour Party (UK)|British Labour Party]] politician [[Dawn Butler]] wrote to the BBC asking for an "urgent explanation" over the racial slur, declaring that it "should never have been aired" and describing the broadcast as "painful and unforgivable".<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cn712kvkreeo |title=Labour MP Dawn Butler asks BBC for explanation over racial slur during BAFTAs broadcast |last=Saunders |first=Emma |publisher=[[BBC]] |date=24 February 2026 |access-date=24 February 2026}}</ref> [[New York City Public Advocate]] [[Jumaane Williams]], the city's first publicly elected official with Tourette syndrome, released a video the following day in which he expressed disappointment that BAFTA did not better support all parties involved in the debacle, stating: "BAFTA could have censored the damn thing." He also urged people to have compassion for Davidson, emphasising that he had every right to attend a ceremony where a film about his life was being celebrated, adding, "We have to make sure we're ... putting the anger in the right places, but also providing empathy and care for everyone that's involved. And I believe that was dropped when it came to Michael B. Jordan and Delroy Lindo. Shout out to them. Sadly, Black people have to know how to have poise in so many situations just so they can survive."<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.thewrap.com/industry-news/business/bafta-tourette-sinners-n-word-jumaane-williams-reaction/ |title=NYC Public Advocate Jumaane Williams, Who Has Tourette's, Says 'BAFTA Could Have Censored the Damn Thing' |last=Schwartz |first=Missy |work=[[TheWrap]] |date=24 February 2026 |access-date=24 February 2026}}</ref>

====Aftermath====
Davidson later released a statement, explaining his outbursts:
{{blockquote|I wanted to thank BAFTA and everyone involved in the awards last night for their support and understanding and inviting me to attend the broadcast. I appreciated the announcement to the auditorium in advance of the recording, warning everyone that my tics are involuntary and are not a reflection of my personal beliefs. I was heartened by the round of applause that followed this announcement and felt welcomed and understood in an environment that would normally be impossible for me. In addition to the announcement by Alan Cumming, the BBC and BAFTA, I can only add that I am, and always have been deeply mortified if anyone considers my involuntary tics to be intentional or to carry any meaning. I was in attendance to celebrate the film of my life, ''I Swear'', which more than any film or TV documentary, explains the origins, condition, traits and manifestations of Tourette Syndrome. I have spent my life trying to support and empower the Tourette's community and to teach empathy, kindness and understanding from others and I will continue to do so. I chose to leave the auditorium early into the ceremony as I was aware of the distress my tics were causing.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2026/02/john-davidson-issues-statement-bafta-racial-slur-i-swear-1236733373/ |title=John Davidson Says He is 'Deeply Mortified' That His Tourettes Tics Could Be Seen as 'Intentional' in First Statement After BAFTA Film Awards |last1=Goldbart |first1=Max |last2=Ntim |first2=Zac |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026 |archive-date=23 February 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260223185938/https://deadline.com/2026/02/john-davidson-issues-statement-bafta-racial-slur-i-swear-1236733373/ |url-status=live}}</ref>}}

BAFTA also released a full statement, addressing the incident and apologising:
{{blockquote|We take the duty of care to all our guests very seriously and start from a position of inclusion. We took measures to make those in attendance aware of the tics, announcing to the audience before the ceremony began, and throughout, that John was in the room and that they may hear strong language, involuntary noises or movements during the ceremony. Early in the ceremony, a loud tic in the form of a profoundly offensive term was heard by many people in the room. Michael B. Jordan and Delroy Lindo were on stage at the time, and we apologise unreservedly to them, and to all those impacted. We would like to thank Michael and Delroy for their incredible dignity and professionalism.<ref>{{cite press release |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/a-statement-from-bafta/ |title=A Statement from BAFTA |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts|BAFTA]] |date=23 February 2026 |access-date=23 February 2026}}</ref>}}

On 24{{nbsp}}February, BAFTA announced that a "comprehensive review is underway" in a letter to members whose language significantly overlapped the previous statement.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2026/film/awards/bafta-comprehensive-review-awards-n-word-incident-1236671404/ |title=BAFTA Says a 'Comprehensive Review is Underway' Over Film Awards N-Word Incident in Letter to Members: 'Our Intention to Be Inclusive in No Way Diminishes the Impact' |last=Ritman |first=Alex |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=24 February 2026 |access-date=24 February 2026}}</ref>

Speaking with ''Variety'', during his first interview following the backlash, Davidson echoed the many commentators who had suggested that his use of the N-word should have been edited out of the broadcast. He also claimed that he was told before the ceremony that any profanity would be excluded and expressed surprise that a broadcast partner that had previously covered his condition extensively was not better prepared for potential incidents.<ref name="Interview">{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2026/film/awards/john-davidson-tourettes-tics-bafta-n-word-interview-1236671850/ |title=John Davidson Gives First Interview and Explains Tourette's Tics After Shouting N-Word and Other Slurs at BAFTAs: 'I Felt a Wave of Shame' (Exclusive) |last=Davis |first=Clayton |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=24 February 2026 |access-date=24 February 2026 |archive-date=2 March 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260302201434/https://variety.com/2026/film/awards/john-davidson-tourettes-tics-bafta-n-word-interview-1236671850/ |url-status=live}}</ref>

"[[StudioCanal]] were working closely with BAFTA and BAFTA had made us all aware that any swearing would be edited out of the broadcast", Davidson said, adding, "I have made four documentaries with the BBC in the past, and feel that they should have been aware of what to expect from Tourette's and worked harder to prevent anything that I said — which, after all, was some 40 rows back from the stage — from being included in the broadcast."<ref name="Interview"/>

While Davidson maintained that Tourette syndrome should not bar anyone from attending award shows or other public events, he also questioned the decision to seat him near a microphone, which could have amplified his outbursts that might have otherwise gone unnoticed by people onstage and watching at home. "As I reflect on the auditorium, I remember there was a microphone just in front of me", he said. "With hindsight I have to question whether this was wise, so close to where I was seated, knowing I would tic." He concluded the interview by stating that "it's important not to use the word 'disability'. This is considered a 'condition' by the Tourette's community".<ref name="Interview"/>

On 28{{nbsp}}February, during the [[57th NAACP Image Awards]], presented by the American-based National Association for the Advancement of Colored People (NAACP), actress [[Regina Hall]] gave Jordan and Lindo a special shout-out before presenting the first award of the night. Shifting attention to the duo, Hall gestured towards Jordan and Lindo, and asked the audience to "take a moment for the two kings who are in this audience", adding, "[I] just send you so much love for your class." Those in attendance subsequently gave both actors a standing ovation to salute them. Taking to the stage to present an award with ''Sinners'' filmmaker [[Ryan Coogler]] later during the evening, both received a standing ovation. "I appreciate, we appreciate, all the support and the love that we have been shown in the aftermath of what happened last weekend. It means a lot to us", Lindo said, adding, "It is an honor to be here amongst our people ... It's a classic case of something that could be very negative becoming very positive."<ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2026/awards/news/naacp-image-awards-michael-b-jordan-delroy-lindo-bafta-1236675587/ |title=Delroy Lindo Thanks Supporters After BAFTA N-Word Incident: 'It's a Classic Case of Something That Could Be Very Negative Becoming Very Positive' |last=Jackson |first=Angelique |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=28 February 2026 |access-date=28 February 2026 |archive-date=1 March 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260301031956/https://variety.com/2026/awards/news/naacp-image-awards-michael-b-jordan-delroy-lindo-bafta-1236675587/ |url-status=live}}</ref>

At the same event, host [[Deon Cole]] targeted Davidson with a joke, concerning his condition and the slur itself, that the audience enjoyed. "If there are any white men out here in the audience, Lord, with Tourette's, I advise you to tell them they'd better read the room tonight, Lord", Cole said in a mock prayer, adding, "It might not go the way they thinketh. Whatever medicine they're on, they'd better double up on it, Lord."<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.the-independent.com/arts-entertainment/tv/news/naacp-awards-tourettes-john-deon-cole-b2930047.html |title=Comedian addresses backlash after making joke about John Davidson's Tourette's racial slur |last=Stolworthy |first=Jacob |newspaper=[[The Independent]] |date=2 March 2026 |access-date=3 March 2026}}</ref>

On 2{{nbsp}}March, a week after the ceremony, Cumming posted a lengthier apology on social media, writing: "What should've been an evening celebrating creativity as well as diversity and inclusion turned into a trauma triggering shitshow. I'm so sorry for all the pain Black people have felt at hearing that word echoed around the world. I'm so sorry the Tourettes community has been reminded of the lack of understanding and tolerance that abounds regarding their condition." Cumming further criticised the BBC's decision "to both broadcast slurs and censor free speech" before congratulating the ceremony's winners.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2026/tv/news/bafta-alan-cumming-apology-john-davidson-n-word-1236677734/ |title=BAFTA Host Alan Cumming Apologizes After John Davidson's N-Word Outburst: 'A Trauma Triggering S—show' |last=Dunn |first=Jack |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=2 March 2026 |access-date=3 March 2026}}</ref>

''Deadline Hollywood'' also reported that a "difficult" meeting between the BBC and Warner Bros. had taken place. Warner Bros. executives were said to have pressed the BBC for answers about why the racial slur made the final cut, despite the BAFTAs being recorded two hours before broadcasting on television. Questions were also raised about why the incident remained on iPlayer for fifteen hours, even though the BBC was aware that the slur was audible. They also demanded to know what steps the BBC would take to prevent a similar incident from happening again. One person briefed on the encounter said that "they were furious". Warner executives had initially sought a meeting with the BBC on the Monday following the ceremony, but were left "frustrated" when the gathering did not materialise.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2026/03/warner-bros-fury-bbc-bafta-debacle-tense-meeting-1236744940/ |title=Warner Bros. Vented Anger at BBC Over BAFTA N-Word Debacle During Tense Meeting |last=Kanter |first=Jake |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=6 March 2026 |access-date=6 March 2026}}</ref>

On 6{{nbsp}}March, following the BBC's review and investigation, it was announced that [[Director-General of the BBC]] [[Tim Davie]] answered questions from the UK government's [[Culture, Media and Sport Committee]] over the incident. In a letter to committee chair Dame [[Caroline Dinenage]], Davie explained why the N-word outburst was not edited out, asserting that it was a "genuine mistake" and the BBC takes "full responsibility" for it. He also wrote that the BBC's "initial evidence gathering" found that no one in the on-site broadcast truck heard the initial outburst when they were watching the live feed and that "there was therefore no editorial decision made to leave the language in". However, he noted that a second outburst of the word occurred, while [[Wunmi Mosaku]] accepted her award for [[BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Supporting Role|Best Actress in a Supporting Role]], writing: "In that instance, the edit team did hear the racial slur on the feed and removed it immediately from the version of the ceremony that would be broadcast later that evening. This was in line with protocols and procedures that were in place for this event." The mistake occurred, he claimed, when the edit team started received reports about a racial slur, "including from BAFTA", explaining, "Our understanding at this point is that the team editing the show in the truck mistakenly believed they had edited out the incident that was being referenced, on the basis that they had heard and edited out the slur shouted out during the best supporting actress award. Therefore, when they were told a racial slur had been shouted, they believed they had removed it."<ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2026/tv/global/bbc-boss-says-bafta-n-word-incident-was-genuine-mistake-1236680882/ |title=BBC Boss Says BAFTA N-Word Controversy 'Was a Genuine Mistake': We 'Will Learn Lessons from This Incident' |last=Ritman |first=Alex |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=6 March 2026 |access-date=6 March 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.com/mediacentre/2026/statements/bbc-letter-to-culture-media-and-sport-select-committee-bafta-broadcast |title=BBC letter to Culture Media and Sport Select Committee in relation to BAFTAs broadcast |publisher=[[BBC]] |date=6 March 2026 |access-date=6 March 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2026/03/bbc-sets-out-n-word-debacle-bafta-film-awards-1236745833/ |title=BBC Reveals How it Came to Air the N-Word at BAFTA Film Awards and How the Slur Remained on iPlayer for So Long |last=Goldbart |first=Max |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=6 March 2026 |access-date=6 March 2026 |archive-date=6 March 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260306142156/https://deadline.com/2026/03/bbc-sets-out-n-word-debacle-bafta-film-awards-1236745833/ |url-status=live}}</ref>

On 8{{nbsp}}April 2026, the BBC's Executive Complaints Unit (ECU) issued the findings of its investigation.<ref name="ECU">{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/contact/ecu/bafta-film-awards-2026-bbc-one-and-bbc-iplayer |title=BAFTA Film Awards 2026, BBC One and BBC iPlayer |publisher=[[BBC]] |date=8 April 2026 |access-date=8 April 2026 |archive-date=8 April 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260408202309/https://www.bbc.co.uk/contact/ecu/bafta-film-awards-2026-bbc-one-and-bbc-iplayer |url-status=live}}</ref> According to the ECU, the inclusion of Davidson's outburst in the broadcast "had no editorial justification and represented a [[Breach of contract|breach]] of the BBC's editorial standards, but that the breach was unintentional". Actions were also taken to prevent future breaches, and letters of apology were sent to Davidson, Jordan and Lindo.<ref name="ECU"/><ref>{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2026/04/bbc-bafta-n-word-broadcast-breached-editorial-standards-1236784469/ |title=BAFTA N-Word Broadcast Breached Editorial Standards But Was 'Unintentional', BBC Rules |last=Goldbart |first=Max |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=8 April 2026 |access-date=8 April 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.theguardian.com/media/2026/apr/08/airing-of-bafta-racial-slur-breached-bbc-standards-corporation-finds |title=Airing of BAFTA racial slur breached BBC standards, corporation finds |last=Davies |first=Caroline |newspaper=[[The Guardian]] |date=8 April 2026 |access-date=8 April 2026}}</ref>
